chunks.text: Alistair Sinclair is a Professor in the EECS department at UC Berkeley. Contact information includes office at 677 Soda Hall, phone 510-643-8144, email sinclair@cs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are Monday 1:30-2:30 and Tuesday 12:45-1:45 at 677 Soda. Natalie Chen (nchen86@berkeley.edu) provides research support.

Alistair Sinclair received a B.A. in Mathematics from St. John's College, University of Cambridge in 1979 and a Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Edinburgh in 1988. Before joining UC Berkeley, he held faculty positions at Edinburgh and visiting positions at DIMACS and the International Computer Science Institute in Berkeley. He received the Goedel Prize in 1996 and the Fulkerson Prize in 2006.

Research areas include Theory (THY) with focus on randomized algorithms, applied probability, and statistical physics. Affiliated research centers are the Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB) and the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC).
Teaching schedule includes CS 70: Discrete Mathematics and Probability Theory, held TuTh 12:30-13:59 in Dwinelle 155.

Selected publications include: 'Glauber dynamics on trees: Boundary conditions and mixing time' (2004), 'A computational view of population genetics' (1998), 'Polynomial-time approximation algorithms for the Ising model' (1993), and 'Quadratic dynamical systems' (1992).

Selected honors and awards include: STOC Test of Time Award (2021), ACM SIGACT Distinguished Service Prize (2017), ACM Fellow (2012), Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching (2012), IMS Medallion Lecturer (2008), Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching (2007), Fulkerson Prize (2006), Okawa Research Grant (2002), and Gödel Prize (1996).
